personalityQuirks: Constantly adjusts their hair or clothing — deeply self-conscious about appearance
Speaks to animals as if they understand complex sentences
Refuses to make direct eye contact — an old habit from living near medusas
Collects small stones and arranges them in patterns when nervous
Hums funeral dirges under their breath without realizing it
Tastes the air like a snake before entering any new room
Carries a mirror and checks it compulsively — terrified of petrification
Speaks only in whispers inside the keep, convinced the walls listen
Apologizes to inanimate objects when bumping into them
Flinches at sudden movements — has been ambushed too many times

battleCries.cry: "Guests are always welcome to stay. Intruders stay forever."
battleCries.narrative: Lady Emer's voice rings through the keep, equal parts invitation and death sentence.
Emer's golden eyes blaze with petrifying power as she locks gazes with her target.
A cold promise — Lady Emer is fulfilling a contract, and she never fails.

battleCries.cry: "You escaped fire giants. Let me show you something colder."
battleCries.narrative: Lady Emer acknowledges the hunters' past with a predator's respect.

battleCries.cry: "Hold still. This will only hurt until it doesn't."
battleCries.narrative: Emer's voice is almost gentle as stone begins to crawl up her target's limbs.

battleCries.cry: "My garden needs new statues. You'll look lovely in the spring."
battleCries.narrative: The medusa gestures at her petrified collection with genuine artistic pride.

environmentalEvents.name: Ceiling Collapse
environmentalEvents.narrative: A section of the keep's ancient ceiling gives way. Chunks of stone and a shower of snow crash down.
environmentalEvents.mechanic: DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or 2d6 bludgeoning damage. Area becomes difficult terrain.

environmentalEvents.name: Flemgot Spore Cloud
environmentalEvents.narrative: A gust of wind disturbs a moldy tapestry, releasing a cloud of mind-altering spores.
environmentalEvents.mechanic: DC 16 Constitution saving throw or poisoned for 10 minutes (disadvantage on saves vs. frightened).

environmentalEvents.name: Chandelier Flicker
environmentalEvents.narrative: The enchanted chandeliers flicker wildly, casting dancing shadows that briefly make every surface look like it's moving.
environmentalEvents.mechanic: DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or frightened for 1 round (illusion magic).

environmentalEvents.name: Basilisk Tracks
environmentalEvents.narrative: Fresh claw marks in the stone — eight-legged, deep grooves. Something large passed through recently.
environmentalEvents.mechanic: No mechanical effect. Warns of basilisks nearby.

environmentalEvents.name: Serpent Hair Hiss
environmentalEvents.narrative: From somewhere in the keep, you hear the distinctive hissing of dozens of small serpents. Lady Emer is nearby.
environmentalEvents.mechanic: No mechanical effect. Alerts party to Lady Emer's proximity.

environmentalEvents.name: Frozen Wind Blast
environmentalEvents.narrative: A gust of freezing wind screams through a gap in the keep's walls, carrying stinging ice crystals.
environmentalEvents.mechanic: DC 12 Constitution saving throw or 1d4 cold damage and disadvantage on next attack roll.

environmentalEvents.name: Crumbling Wall
environmentalEvents.narrative: A section of wall gives way under the slightest pressure, revealing a narrow passage — or a 60-foot drop.
environmentalEvents.mechanic: DC 14 Dexterity saving throw to avoid falling if leaning against the wall.

environmentalEvents.name: Illusory Sound
environmentalEvents.narrative: The keep's residual illusion magic creates a phantom sound — footsteps, whispered conversation, or a scream from an empty room.
environmentalEvents.mechanic: DC 15 Investigation to identify as illusory. Can lure party into ambush position.

texts: Your breath comes in sharp, thin gasps. The air at this altitude barely fills your lungs.
Ice crusts your eyelashes. Every blink is a conscious effort.
The cold doesn't bite — it seeps. It finds every gap in your armor, every patch of exposed skin.
Snow whips through holes in the keep's ceiling, dusting the stone floors with white powder that never seems to melt.
The statue's expression is frozen mid-scream. Whoever this was, they died terrified.
Stone dust coats everything. Some of it was walls once. Some of it was people.
You catch yourself avoiding the statues' eyes, as if they might still carry the medusa's curse.
The stone garden's flowers grow from the cracks in petrified flesh. Beauty feeding on horror.
A quill lies on the floor, broken mid-word. The poetry it was writing is terrible.
Serpent scales glint in the chandelier light — shed like hair, everywhere Emer walks.
The perfume is strongest here. She passed through recently.
A half-finished poem on the desk: 'The stone remembers what the flesh forgets / And I remember everything.'
The walls shimmer at the edges of your vision. This keep was built by someone who didn't trust reality.
A shadow moves where nothing casts one. The illusionist's magic is fading, but not gone.
The chandeliers cast colored light that shouldn't be possible from simple flames.
For a moment, the wall looks paper-thin. Then solid stone again. You're not sure which was the illusion.
